definition: Fiscal deficits amplify commodity-to-inflation passthrough by sustaining household incomes and corporate profits, preventing the demand destruction that historically self-corrected commodity price spikes. This makes the sacrifice ratio endogenous to fiscal policy — a feature not captured in Taylor Rule frameworks.
evidence: Kalecki logic: deficits at 6-7% GDP sustain demand and prevent self-correction
Sacrifice ratio endogeneity to fiscal policy not captured in standard Taylor Rule frameworks
Directional claim confirmed in pair_0 debate; specific 3-4x quantification refuted as crude linear scaling
Connection to KB concept fiscal_deficit_kalecki_channel (confidence 9) provides theoretical grounding
open_questions: What is the correct fiscal multiplier for commodity passthrough amplification specifically?
Does deficit composition (transfers vs investment vs interest) matter more than headline ratio?
How does the interaction between fiscal and monetary policy affect the amplification channel?
